name: Gryphon
description: The Gryphon, also spelled Griffon or Griffin, is a legendary creature with the body, tail, and back legs of a lion; the head and wings of an eagle; and sometimes an eagle's talons as its front feet. Because the lion was traditionally considered the king of the beasts and the eagle the king of birds, the gryphon was thought to be an especially powerful and majestic creature. Gryphons were often depicted as guardians of treasure and priceless possessions. They were associated with the sun, Apollo, and Nemesis. Their ferocity and strength made them formidable protectors, and their keen eyesight allowed them to spot threats from great distances. Some accounts even attribute them with the ability to find gold and other precious metals, further solidifying their role as guardians of wealth. Their image has been used throughout history in heraldry and art, symbolizing courage, strength, and vigilance. They are fiercely loyal and protective of their territory and offspring.
subtitle: Lion-Eagle Hybrid: Guardian of Treasure
appearance: The Gryphon possesses the body, tail, and hind legs of a lion, typically depicted with golden or tawny fur. Its forelimbs are replaced by the wings and head of an eagle, complete with sharp beak and piercing eyes. The feathers are often a mix of brown, gold, and white. Some depictions include feathered legs in place of the lion's forelegs, ending in sharp talons. Their overall size is significantly larger than either an eagle or a lion, making them imposing figures.
habitat: Gryphons are often found in mountainous regions, nesting on high cliffs or in caves. They are also associated with deserts and areas known for their mineral wealth, reflecting their role as guardians of treasure. Their territories are typically vast, allowing them ample space to hunt and patrol.
behavior: Gryphons are fiercely territorial and protective of their nests and offspring. They are solitary creatures, except during mating season. They are intelligent and cunning hunters, using their keen eyesight and aerial abilities to spot prey from great distances. They are known for their bravery and unwillingness to back down from a challenge, making them formidable opponents. They are also considered noble creatures, embodying strength and vigilance.

keyMyths.title: Guardians of Apollo's Gold
keyMyths.description: Gryphons were said to guard Apollo's gold in Scythia, fiercely protecting it from any who dared to steal it. Their vigilance and strength made them nearly impossible to overcome. This association cemented their role as guardians of valuable possessions.

keyMyths.title: Conflict with Arimaspians
keyMyths.description: Gryphons were constantly at odds with the Arimaspians, a one-eyed tribe who sought to steal the gold the Gryphons guarded. These conflicts were legendary, highlighting the Gryphons' unwavering dedication to their duty. The Arimaspians were said to constantly try and steal the gold.

sources.author: Aeschylus
sources.description: Play referencing the gryphons guarding Apollo's gold.
sources.text: Play referencing the gryphons guarding Apollo's gold.

sources.author: Herodotus
sources.description: Detailed account of the conflict between Gryphons and Arimaspians.

sources.author: Pliny the Elder
sources.description: Describes the Gryphon as a real animal found in Scythia.
